title: First Coal
description: The ritual fire is lit from a single ember — a slow filtered kick and a low drone breathe together while distant taiko taps mark time like a heartbeat finding its rhythm.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-1:00, sub drone + heavily filtered kick, no percussion) -> Section A (0:60-2:00, single taiko hit every 4 bars, filter opens slowly) -> Section B (2:00-3:30, hand percussion loop enters, bassline outline appears under filter) -> Lift (3:30-4:15, filter sweep opens fully, sparse acid line introduced) -> Outro (4:15-5:00, elements drop out one by one back to drone)
soundDesignPrompt: Single low sine/saw blend sub drone tuned to F, slow 24dB low-pass filter automation on the kick, one resonant taiko sample with long natural decay and subtle pitch-down on each hit, granular texture made from reversed nylon-guitar pluck stretched to 8 seconds for ghost shimmer underneath everything.
mixMasterNotes: Keep sub energy mono below 100Hz so the drone doesn't smear on club subs; leave generous headroom (-9 LUFS target territory for this track) since it's a true intro — let dynamics breathe rather than compressing flat.
djTransitionNotes: Long 64-bar intro built for beatmixing in cold; pairs with any 136-140 BPM opener; no hard drop to navigate, safe to mix in over 32 bars and out into track 2 at the lift section.
stylePrompt: Psy-trance ritual opener, 138 BPM, F minor, filtered sub drone, single taiko hit, slow filter-sweep kick, granular nylon-guitar ghost texture, hypnotic minimal build, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A single coal-orange ember pulses at the center of black velvet darkness; thin violet smoke tendrils curl outward in sync with the sub drone, brightening to acid-green only when the taiko hits land, camera-still and meditative.
filmPrompt: Extreme slow push-in toward a single glowing coal in total darkness, smoke curling past the lens in violet haze, no cuts, time feels suspended until the very last frame where the coal flares bright.
imagePrompt: A single glowing ember resting on black velvet cloth in a pitch-dark ritual space, thin violet smoke rising past it, faint chrome mask barely visible at the edge of the frame catching the ember's orange light, cinematic macro lighting, painterly realism, no text, no faces

title: Salt Circle
description: A perimeter is drawn in rhythm — interlocking hand-drum patterns circle a rolling psy-bass that stays low and patient, like dancers pacing the edge of a space before stepping inside it.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-0:40, four-on-floor kick + closed hi-hat only) -> Build 1 (0:40-1:50, rolling psy-bass enters at low filter, conga/djembe interlock pattern layers in) -> Drop 1 (1:50-3:10, full circular percussion groove with acid bass barely cracked open) -> Breakdown (3:10-4:00, percussion solo, kick removed, metallic delay taps) -> Build 2 (4:00-4:40, kick returns, tension riser) -> Outro (4:40-5:20, percussion thins back to single djembe and fade)
soundDesignPrompt: 303-style acid bass kept dark and round with minimal resonance (filter rarely above 40%), layered djembe and low conga one-shots panned in a slow rotating LFO pattern across the stereo field to suggest circling motion, metallic delay taps using a detuned cowbell sample with long ping-pong feedback.
mixMasterNotes: Use the rotating percussion pan automation carefully — keep kick and sub locked dead-center mono while letting the circling drums use the wide stereo field; moderate compression on the bass bus to keep the rolling pattern consistent without flattening transients.
djTransitionNotes: 140 BPM bridges cleanly from track 1's 138; breakdown at 3:10 is the ideal mix-out point with just percussion and delay taps exposed; 40-bar intro for beatmatching in.
stylePrompt: Tribal psy-trance, 140 BPM, D minor, rolling 303 acid bass, circling djembe and conga interlock, metallic cowbell delay, patient warm-up groove, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A ring of acid-green particle dots rotates slowly around a violet void, firelight-orange sparks ignite at each djembe hit along the circle's edge, the ring tightens and widens breathing with the rolling bassline.
filmPrompt: Slow circular tracking shot around a ring of bare footprints drawn in salt on black stone floor, laser haze drifting low across the ground, camera completes one full rotation over the track's first minute.
imagePrompt: An overhead view of a ritual circle traced in white salt on black stone, faint violet laser haze hanging low across the ground, a single chrome tribal mask resting at the circle's edge catching firelight, cinematic top-down composition, no text, no faces

title: Skin and Static
description: Body heat meets electricity — close, dry tribal toms rub against a crackling sub-bass texture that hisses like static building before a storm, the last hypnotic step before the voltage rises.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-0:35, dry tom loop, no bass) -> Build 1 (0:35-1:40, sub-bass crackle texture enters under toms, kick locks in) -> Drop 1 (1:40-3:00, full tom-driven groove with acid line threading through) -> Breakdown (3:00-3:50, all percussion mutes except one tom, static crackle swells, riser begins) -> Build 2 (3:50-4:30, snare-roll-style tom fill rebuilds energy) -> Outro (4:30-5:10, crackle texture alone, fades to silence)
soundDesignPrompt: Dry, close-mic'd tribal tom samples with minimal room reverb for an intimate, physical feel, sub-bass layer built from bit-crushed white noise filtered down to a low rumble for the 'static' character, acid line uses a narrow bandpass sweep rather than full resonance for a thinner, more tense texture.
mixMasterNotes: Keep the static/crackle texture high-passed below 60Hz to avoid masking the true sub; toms should sit forward and dry in the mix with almost no reverb tail so the track stays physically close and tense rather than spacious.
djTransitionNotes: 141 BPM nudges up from track 2's 140, smooth pitch-bend territory; breakdown at 3:00 with single tom exposed is the natural mix point into track 4's intro; short 24-bar intro keeps warm-up section moving.
stylePrompt: Cinematic tribal psy-trance, 141 BPM, G minor, dry close-mic tribal toms, bit-crushed static sub-bass, narrow acid bandpass sweep, tense pre-build energy, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Cracked chrome-textured skin surface fills the frame, acid-green electric fractures spread across it in sync with the tom hits, violet static sparks flicker at the cracks' edges, motion sharp and percussive rather than smooth.
filmPrompt: Macro shot of a hand striking a drum skin in slow motion, dust and static sparks jumping from the impact point, lit only by flickering firelight against total black background.
imagePrompt: Extreme close-up of a tribal drum skin stretched taut, faint electric-looking cracks of acid-green light running across its surface, lit by a single firelight source against black velvet background, hyper-detailed texture, painterly realism, no text, no faces

title: Voltage Marrow
description: The current moves inward — a thick, snarling psy-bass digs into the low-mid range like it's traveling through bone, while taiko hits land with sudden architectural weight.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-0:30, kick + sub pulse) -> Build 1 (0:30-1:30, snarling acid bass enters at mid resonance, taiko hits punctuate every 8 bars) -> Drop 1 (1:30-3:00, full tribal-drive groove, bass fully open) -> Breakdown (3:00-3:45, cinematic riser, taiko solo over sub pulse) -> Build 2 (3:45-4:25, bass re-enters distorted and louder) -> Drop 2 (4:25-5:30, peak of the building section, all layers) -> Outro (5:30-6:00, filtered fade)
soundDesignPrompt: Saw-based 303-style bass with heavier saturation and a resonant low-pass filter swept in the 200-600Hz range for a 'guttural' rather than bright acid tone, taiko samples layered with a sub-octave synth hit underneath each strike for architectural weight, riser built from a slowly rising filtered noise sweep crossfaded with a bowed metal texture.
mixMasterNotes: Apply parallel saturation to the bass bus to keep the guttural growl audible on small systems without losing low-end control on a full club rig; sidechain the bass gently to the kick to avoid buildup in the 60-90Hz pocket where both compete.
djTransitionNotes: 142 BPM continues the building-section climb; breakdown at 3:00 offers an early mix-out option, full breakdown at the 3:45-4:25 build is the stronger transition point; 32-bar outro for clean handoff to track 5.
stylePrompt: Tribal psy-trance, 142 BPM, F minor, guttural saturated 303 bass, sub-octave taiko hits, bowed-metal riser texture, heavy building-section energy, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A pulsing violet mass at the screen's center contracts and expands like a beating organ, acid-green veins of light spread outward with each bass hit, firelight-orange flashes burst on every taiko strike, motion heavy and muscular.
filmPrompt: Low slow-motion tracking shot through smoke toward a chrome tribal mask lit from below by firelight, the mask's surface seems to pulse and breathe in time with the bass, camera never stops moving forward.
imagePrompt: A chrome tribal mask half-submerged in violet smoke, lit from below by a single fire source, its surface reflecting distorted firelight in acid-green and orange streaks, dark cinematic composition, painterly realism, no text, no visible human face beneath the mask

title: Spiral Gait
description: Feet find a rolling, off-kilter rhythm — a syncopated acid line spirals upward in pitch over a steady tribal stomp, capturing the lurching, trance-locked walk of a body giving in.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-0:30, stomp kick + syncopated shaker pattern) -> Build 1 (0:30-1:40, spiraling acid line enters with rising filter automation) -> Drop 1 (1:40-3:00, full spiral groove, tribal stomp locked in) -> Breakdown (3:00-3:40, acid line isolated and pitched up an octave over riser) -> Build 2 (3:40-4:20, stomp returns, syncopation intensifies) -> Drop 2 (4:20-5:40, peak spiral pattern with full percussion stack) -> Outro (5:40-6:10, filtered wind-down)
soundDesignPrompt: Acid line programmed with an asymmetric 3-3-2 step pattern rather than straight 16ths to create the 'off-kilter' spiral feel, filter cutoff modulated by a slow sine LFO synced to 8 bars for the rising-spiral sensation, tribal stomp built from layered low floor-tom and foot-stamp field recording for a grounded, physical low end.
mixMasterNotes: Watch phase correlation on the layered stomp samples — sum to mono periodically while mixing to confirm the low end stays solid; the acid line's filter sweep should be automated with enough headroom that the resonant peak never clips even at the top of the spiral.
djTransitionNotes: Same 142 BPM as track 4 for a seamless phrase-matched transition; the isolated acid-line breakdown at 3:00 is a strong drop-out point for creative DJ layering; 32-bar intro and outro both built for tight beatmatching.
stylePrompt: Psy-trance, 142 BPM, C minor, asymmetric spiraling acid line, layered tribal floor-tom stomp, rising LFO filter sweep, off-kilter trance-lock groove, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A glowing acid-green spiral unwinds continuously from the center of the frame against deep violet, rotating and rising in scale with the filter sweep, firelight-orange embers trail behind the spiral's outer edge like sparks off a spinning blade.
filmPrompt: Camera spirals slowly upward around a rising column of smoke and laser haze, fragments of chrome mask imagery glimpsed only in flashes between the smoke, disorienting but rhythmic motion.
imagePrompt: A spiral of laser haze and smoke rising through darkness, lit in alternating violet and acid-green light beams, a chrome ornamental fragment catching firelight at the spiral's base, abstract cinematic composition, painterly realism, no text, no faces

title: Chrome Totem
description: A towering, metallic lead carved from delay and feedback stands at the center of the groove like a totem reflecting firelight — rigid, gleaming, and impossible to ignore.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-0:30, kick + metallic delay tap pattern) -> Build 1 (0:30-1:30, totem lead enters single-note, delay feedback builds) -> Drop 1 (1:30-2:50, full groove with totem lead phrase looping) -> Breakdown (2:50-3:40, lead isolated with extreme delay feedback over riser, percussion drops) -> Build 2 (3:40-4:20, percussion rebuilds under sustained lead) -> Drop 2 (4:20-5:30, peak totem-lead pattern, busiest arrangement of the building section) -> Outro (5:30-6:00, delay tail rings out alone)
soundDesignPrompt: Totem lead built from a metallic FM synth tone (bell-like operator ratio) run through a long ping-pong delay with feedback pushed near self-oscillation at the breakdown, taiko and tribal tom layer underneath kept drier to contrast against the wet, ringing lead, kick uses a tight transient-shaped sample to stay punchy despite the dense metallic frequencies above it.
mixMasterNotes: Carve a notch in the lead's EQ around 2-4kHz where the kick's click lives to avoid masking; automate delay feedback send level rather than leaving it static so the totem effect breathes instead of becoming a constant wash.
djTransitionNotes: 143 BPM is the building section's energy peak before the peak-time block; the breakdown at 2:50 with isolated ringing lead is ideal for a long blend into track 7; 32-bar structure throughout for clean mixing.
stylePrompt: Cinematic tribal psy-trance, 143 BPM, A minor, metallic FM bell lead, self-oscillating ping-pong delay, dry tribal tom contrast, totemic intense build, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A tall chrome totem shape assembles itself from floating geometric shards against black velvet, each shard locking into place on a delay-tap hit, acid-green light traces the totem's edges while violet haze swirls around its base.
filmPrompt: Slow vertical tilt up the length of a towering chrome ritual totem catching firelight at its base and laser haze near its top, smoke drifting horizontally across the frame throughout.
imagePrompt: A tall ritual totem made of polished chrome fragments standing in black velvet darkness, its surface fractured into mask-like patterns reflecting firelight orange at the base fading to violet laser haze near the top, cinematic vertical composition, painterly realism, no text, no faces

title: Blood Rhythm Overdrive
description: Pulse and percussion fully merge — overdriven tribal drums and a distorted rolling bass lock into a single relentless heartbeat pattern, the last surge of pressure before the ritual peaks.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-0:25, distorted kick alone) -> Build 1 (0:25-1:20, overdriven tribal toms lock to kick, bass enters muted) -> Drop 1 (1:20-2:40, full overdrive groove, bass opens) -> Breakdown (2:40-3:30, massive cinematic riser, all drums cut to a single sub pulse mimicking a heartbeat) -> Build 2 (3:30-4:10, drums re-enter at double-time fill before drop) -> Drop 2 (4:10-5:40, maximum pressure of the building section, full stack distorted) -> Outro (5:40-6:10, fast filtered cut to silence, priming the peak-time block)
soundDesignPrompt: Kick and tribal drum bus run through matched analog-style tape saturation for unified overdrive character, bassline doubled with a sub-octave sine layer so distortion on the main bass doesn't thin out the low end, heartbeat breakdown built from a single sine sub pulse with slight tempo-synced volume swell mimicking a resting pulse rate before it doubles into the next build.
mixMasterNotes: Master bus limiting should be set conservatively here — this track runs hottest of the building section, so leave at least 1dB true-peak headroom to survive club system processing without pumping artifacts; check the overdrive doesn't introduce harshness above 8kHz that fatigues dancers over a long set.
djTransitionNotes: 144 BPM sits right at the door of the peak-time block; the heartbeat breakdown at 2:40 is a dramatic, DJ-friendly drop-out moment; short 16-bar outro deliberately cuts fast to set up a hard transition into track 8's peak-time opener.
stylePrompt: Tribal psy-trance, 144 BPM, E minor, tape-saturated overdriven drums, doubled sub-octave bass, heartbeat-pulse breakdown, relentless climactic build, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A pulsing acid-green waveform shaped like a heartbeat monitor line cuts across violet smoke, firelight-orange shockwaves ripple outward from the center on every overdriven drum hit, intensity and shake increasing toward a tight, anticipatory stillness at the breakdown.
filmPrompt: Rapid handheld-style push through dense smoke and laser haze toward an unseen central fire source, motion blur increasing until the breakdown moment where everything freezes on a single firelit silhouette.
imagePrompt: A blurred rush of violet laser haze and firelight streaks converging toward a single bright point in total darkness, faint chrome mask fragments caught mid-motion at the edges, high-energy cinematic composition, painterly realism, no text, no faces

title: Voltage Altar
description: The ritual reaches its altar — a screaming high-resonance acid lead tears across a maximal tribal-psy groove, every drum and bass layer locked into the night's first full-force drop.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-0:20, full kick + tribal percussion stack already running) -> Drop 1 (0:20-2:00, maximal groove, screaming acid lead enters at full resonance) -> Breakdown (2:00-2:50, brief cinematic riser, percussion thins to taiko + sub) -> Build (2:50-3:30, rapid re-stack of full percussion) -> Drop 2 (3:30-5:20, peak of the entire album, acid lead doubled an octave apart) -> Outro (5:20-5:50, sudden filtered cut for DJ flexibility)
soundDesignPrompt: Acid lead pushed to near-self-oscillating resonance with fast envelope-modulated cutoff for a 'screaming' character, doubled an octave apart in Drop 2 using a slightly detuned second oscillator for width without phasing issues, full tribal percussion stack (taiko, djembe, floor tom, shaker) arranged in interlocking call-and-response pattern rather than a flat loop to keep the maximal section feeling alive rather than cluttered.
mixMasterNotes: This is the album's loudest, densest moment — use multiband compression on the acid lead to tame resonant peaks without losing the scream, and high-pass all percussion layers except kick and floor tom above 150Hz to keep the low end from turning to mud under the dense arrangement.
djTransitionNotes: 144 BPM matches directly from track 7 for a hard, immediate peak-time entry with almost no intro needed; ideal as a 'drop straight in' track for DJs already at full energy; breakdown at 2:00 offers a brief reset point if needed.
stylePrompt: Peak-time psy-trance, 144 BPM, F# minor, screaming self-oscillating acid lead doubled an octave, maximal interlocking tribal percussion stack, full-force altar drop, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A massive acid-green sigil burns into existence across the full frame against black velvet and violet smoke, firelight-orange flames erupt from its edges in sync with the lead's resonant screams, the whole image shudders and strobes on every drum hit.
filmPrompt: Fast low-angle crane shot rising past roaring fire and dense laser haze toward a towering chrome altar structure, strobing light cuts matched to the drop's intensity, no slow moments.
imagePrompt: A chrome ritual altar engulfed in firelight at its base with violet laser haze and acid-green light beams crossing above it, sparks and smoke filling the dark surrounding space, maximal cinematic composition, painterly realism, no text, no faces

title: Taiko Storm Protocol
description: Percussion takes total command — a barrage of layered taiko and tribal toms builds into a polyrhythmic storm while the bassline drops to a stripped, weapon-like pulse beneath it.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-0:20, single taiko already at full volume, no bass) -> Drop 1 (0:20-1:50, polyrhythmic taiko storm builds layer by layer, stripped sub-bass pulse enters last) -> Breakdown (1:50-2:40, all percussion cuts except one taiko line, cinematic riser swells) -> Build (2:40-3:10, rapid taiko re-layering, snare-style roll fill) -> Drop 2 (3:10-5:00, full polyrhythmic storm at maximum density, fastest moment of the album) -> Outro (5:00-5:30, percussion layers peel away in reverse order back to single taiko)
soundDesignPrompt: Three independent taiko/tom layers programmed in 3-against-4 and 5-against-4 polyrhythms that phase against each other rather than locking, sub-bass intentionally stripped to a single mono pulse with no acid filter movement so percussion has total frequency space above it, cinematic riser made from a bowed gong sample stretched and reversed.
mixMasterNotes: With three polyrhythmic percussion layers, use careful transient-focused EQ carving on each (distinct frequency pockets per layer) rather than relying on volume to separate them; the stripped sub pulse should sit centered and unprocessed to act as an anchor under the chaos.
djTransitionNotes: 145 BPM is the album's tempo ceiling — small upward nudge from track 8's 144; the percussion-only intro and outro make this an extremely DJ-friendly track to drop and pull at any point; breakdown at 1:50 is a strong isolated-percussion moment for layering.
stylePrompt: Cinematic tribal psy-trance, 145 BPM, D minor, polyrhythmic taiko and tom storm, stripped mono sub-bass pulse, bowed-gong riser, maximum percussion-driven peak intensity, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Multiple layers of firelight-orange and acid-green shockwave rings expand from different points across the frame at independent, clashing rhythms against deep violet smoke, the screen feels like it's being struck from several directions at once.
filmPrompt: Multi-angle rapid cutting between several ritual drummers' hands striking taiko drums in firelight, smoke and sparks flying with each strike, percussive editing rhythm matched to the polyrhythm itself.
imagePrompt: A cluster of large taiko-style drums arranged around a central fire, sparks flying upward into violet laser haze, each drum surface catching a different colored light — orange, violet, acid-green — dynamic cinematic composition, painterly realism, no text, no faces

title: Full Ritual Engaged
description: Every element of the night collides at once — acid spirals, taiko storm, sub-bass weight, and metallic delay totem fuse into the album's single most complete and exhausting drop.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-0:20, full groove already running, callback to track 5's spiral acid motif) -> Drop 1 (0:20-2:10, all album motifs combined — spiral acid, totem delay lead, taiko layers, rolling bass) -> Breakdown (2:10-3:00, massive riser, near-total silence for two beats before the final build) -> Build (3:00-3:40, fastest snare-style fill fill of the album) -> Drop 2 (3:40-5:40, the album's definitive final peak, every layer at once) -> Outro (5:40-6:20, elements peel away in reverse, ending on the sub drone from track 1 for a full-circle callback)
soundDesignPrompt: Reprises and recombines signature textures from earlier peak tracks — the spiraling 3-3-2 acid pattern from track 5, the FM bell totem lead from track 6, and the polyrhythmic taiko layering from track 9 — arranged so each motif gets a dedicated 8-bar window before all locking together in Drop 2; the two-beat near-silence in the breakdown uses only a faint sub-bass flutter to keep tension alive.
mixMasterNotes: Because this track recombines the densest elements of the album, reference against tracks 8 and 9 during mixdown to ensure consistent loudness and tonal balance rather than letting it become noticeably louder or harsher; this is the climax, not a separate volume spike.
djTransitionNotes: 144 BPM bridges down slightly from track 9's 145; functions well as a 'best of the night' closer to the peak-time block; the two-beat silence in the breakdown is a dramatic, attention-grabbing moment DJs can use for a live mic-free crowd reaction beat; 32-bar outro eases into the comedown block.
stylePrompt: Peak-time cinematic tribal psy-trance, 144 BPM, C minor, combined spiral acid line, FM totem lead, polyrhythmic taiko, rolling sub-bass, full-album climax drop, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Every visual motif from the album converges at once — acid-green spiral, chrome totem shards, firelight shockwaves — orbiting a single violet core that briefly goes to total black during the breakdown before exploding back to full color and motion.
filmPrompt: Sweeping crane shot pulling back from the ritual fire to reveal the full scene — drummers, smoke, chrome masks, laser haze — for the first time in one continuous unbroken take, ending on a freeze at the breakdown's silence.
imagePrompt: A wide cinematic view of a full ritual dance space — central fire, chrome masks catching light, laser haze cutting through violet smoke, scattered taiko drums — composed as the album's most complete visual scene, painterly realism, no text, no faces

title: Ash and Afterimage
description: The fire is dying down — a softened, lower-resonance acid line drifts over a half-time tribal pulse, the body still moving but the mind already drifting toward the exit.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-0:30, half-time kick pattern, warm pad drone) -> Build 1 (0:30-1:40, softened acid line enters at low resonance, taiko pulse slows to half-time feel) -> Drop 1 (1:40-3:00, gentle groove, much sparser than the peak block) -> Breakdown (3:00-3:50, nylon-guitar shimmer texture surfaces briefly above the pad, percussion nearly silent) -> Build 2 (3:50-4:20, percussion returns softly) -> Outro (4:20-5:10, fades into warm drone, percussion thins to nothing)
soundDesignPrompt: Acid line filter resonance reduced significantly compared to peak-block tracks and run through a soft tape-style saturation for warmth rather than aggression, kick pattern shifted to a half-time feel against the still-141-145-range underlying tempo to create a physically slower sensation, brief nylon-guitar shimmer (heavily reverbed, played as slow arpeggiated texture, not a melody) surfaces only in the breakdown as the album's signature ghost-texture moment.
mixMasterNotes: Widen the stereo field on pads and the nylon-guitar shimmer more than earlier tracks since this is a less bass-dominant moment; ease overall loudness down slightly from the peak block so the energy drop is felt physically, not just programmatically.
djTransitionNotes: 140 BPM steps down from track 10's 144 — best mixed with a gradual tempo pull-down over 16-32 bars rather than a hard cut; breakdown at 3:00 with exposed nylon shimmer is a strong spot for a long ambient-style blend into track 12.
stylePrompt: Psy-trance comedown, 140 BPM, B minor, softened low-resonance acid line, half-time tribal pulse, reverbed nylon-guitar shimmer texture, warm afterglow groove, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Glowing acid-green and firelight-orange particles that were sharp and fast in earlier tracks now drift slowly upward like rising ash against deep violet, fading gently at the top of the frame, motion soft and unhurried.
filmPrompt: Slow drifting shot following embers and ash rising from a dying fire into dark empty space, laser haze thinning to almost nothing, the calmest camera movement of the album.
imagePrompt: Embers and soft ash drifting upward from a low dying fire into dark violet-tinted air, a faint chrome mask fragment half-buried in ash nearby, soft warm lighting, painterly and quiet composition, no text, no faces

title: Last Ember Walk
description: Dawn pressure on the horizon — the rolling bass and tribal drums finally rest, leaving only a slow sub pulse, a fading taiko echo, and the night's last nylon-guitar ghost-note before silence.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (0:00-0:45, slow sub pulse alone) -> Section A (0:45-2:00, sparse taiko echo enters at long intervals, faint nylon-guitar ghost-note motif appears once) -> Section B (2:00-3:30, gentle rolling bass returns one final time at low intensity, full but minimal groove) -> Breakdown (3:30-4:20, bass drops away, only taiko echo and drone remain) -> Outro (4:20-5:40, drone and final single taiko hit fade to total silence, mirroring track 1's opening)
soundDesignPrompt: Sub pulse uses the same fundamental tone as track 1's opening drone to bookend the album, taiko sample processed with a long, slowly decaying reverb tail (the only track to use spacious reverb on percussion) to feel like it's echoing into open air rather than a tight ritual space, single nylon-guitar ghost-note motif played once, heavily reverbed and pitched down slightly, as the album's final surfacing of its texture-only guitar element.
mixMasterNotes: Final master should have the most headroom and least limiting of the album to let the fade-to-silence outro feel natural rather than abruptly cut by a brickwall ceiling; ensure the sub pulse frequency matches track 1 closely if sequenced back-to-back for a satisfying full-album loop.
djTransitionNotes: 138 BPM returns to the album's opening tempo, a deliberate full-circle close; long ambient-style outro with no hard rhythmic ending makes this ideal as a final set closer rather than a mix-out point; works best left to play out in full.
stylePrompt: Cinematic psy-trance comedown closer, 138 BPM, A minor, slow sub pulse, reverbed taiko echo, single reverbed nylon-guitar ghost-note, gentle final rolling bass, fading ritual close, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A single firelight-orange ember (matching track 1's visual) glows alone in black velvet darkness, violet smoke thinning gradually, one last acid-green flicker appears briefly before the entire frame fades slowly to black.
filmPrompt: Slow pull-back wide shot revealing the ritual space now empty and quiet, the fire reduced to its last coals, smoke thinning into the dark sky, ending on a held still frame of near-total darkness.
imagePrompt: A nearly extinguished fire's last single ember glowing in an empty ritual space at black velvet darkness, faint violet smoke trailing upward, one chrome mask left resting on the ground beside the ashes, quiet cinematic closing composition, painterly realism, no text, no faces
